codeReview.summary: The feature adds the expected modules (rasterizer, burner, repackager, pipeline orchestrator, filename utility), but redactDocument does not meet the required per-page memory-release behavior.

issues.description: The pipeline accumulates all rendered canvases in memory (`pageCanvases`) and only repackages/releases them after all pages are processed. This violates the sequential render→burn→embed→release requirement and can cause excessive memory growth on multi-page PDFs.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The engine-worker skill is missing explicit guidance that jsdom lacks a real HTMLCanvasElement 2D context and tests must mock canvas APIs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: No jsdom/canvas guidance appears in .factory/skills/engine-worker/SKILL.md. The reviewed transcript skeleton explicitly notes: "jsdom doesn't have a real canvas implementation" and the worker had to rework tests around canvas mocking; handoff.skillFeedback also recommends adding this.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md includes a jsPDF metadata example that sets `producer`, but strict TypeScript typings for jsPDF DocumentProperties omit that field, creating avoidable implementation friction.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md key pattern shows `setProperties({ ..., producer: '' ... })`. In this feature, the handoff records a type mismatch and src/core/redactor/repackager.ts omits `producer` to satisfy TypeScript.

sharedStateObservations.observation: A pdfjs-dist typing quirk (convertToViewportPoint typed as any[] instead of tuple) is not captured in shared library docs, despite requiring a cast in implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/core/pipeline/redact-document.ts casts `renderViewport as unknown as Viewport` before burnRedactions. The handoff documents this as a discovered issue, while .factory/library/environment.md does not mention it.

summary: Fail: the redaction pipeline violates the feature's memory contract by retaining per-page 300 DPI canvases until the end instead of embedding and releasing each page as it is processed.
